description.task: Implement an RbacEngine class that enforces Role-Based Access Control. Users have roles, roles have permissions, and resources require specific permissions. Implement method access checks and a permission inheritance chain (ADMIN inherits all USER permissions).
instructions: assignRole() adds the role to the user's role set (create if absent)
grantPermission() adds the permission to the role's permission set (create if absent)
hasPermission() iterates over all user roles, checks each role's permission set, returns true if any match
allPermissions() collects and returns the union of permissions from all user roles
Return false/empty set for unknown usernames without throwing exceptions
description.hint: For hasPermission(), use userRoles.getOrDefault(username, Set.of()) and stream any-match. For allPermissions(), flatMap the roles to their permissions using stream and collect to Set.
description.expectedOutput:
Alice DELETE: false
Bob DELETE: true
Alice perms: 2
Bob perms: 3

description.task: Build a MethodSecurityInterceptor class that simulates Spring Security's @PreAuthorize annotation behavior. Register methods with required roles; when a method is invoked with a user context, verify the user holds any of the required roles before allowing execution.
instructions: secureMethod() registers the method with its required roles
invoke() checks if the user has at least one role from the method's required roles
If access is denied, throw new SecurityException('Access denied for user: ' + username)
If the method is not registered in methodRoles, allow execution without restriction
description.hint: In invoke(), use Collections.disjoint() negation: if (!Collections.disjoint(userRoles, methodRoles)) allow; else throw. Get the sets with getOrDefault(methodName, Set.of()) to handle unregistered methods.
description.expectedOutput:
Alice viewProfile: profile data
Alice deleteUser: ACCESS DENIED
Bob deleteUser: User deleted

description.task: Implement an OAuth2ScopeValidator class that checks whether an access token's granted scopes are sufficient for a requested resource. Model an authorization server that issues tokens with specific scopes and a resource server that validates them.
instructions: issueToken() stores the AccessToken in the tokens map keyed by tokenId
protectResource() stores the required scopes for the given resource path
authorize() returns false if tokenId is not found
authorize() returns false if currentTime > token.expiresAt()
authorize() returns true only if token scopes contain ALL required scopes for the resource (not just any)
description.hint: For scope check, use token.scopes().containsAll(requiredScopes). Get requiredScopes with resourceScopes.getOrDefault(resourcePath, Set.of()). Check expiry with currentTime > token.expiresAt().
